ucs.title: [LLM] MERCURY / Mango Sandstorm RMM-tool deployment via spear-phish + file-sharing-hosted MSI
ucs.description: Hunts MuddyWater's signature post-2022 intrusion playbook: spear-phish links to a public file-sharing service (OneHub, Egnyte, Sync, Onedrive, Dropbox, file.io, etc.) drop a benign-signed Remote Monitoring & Management (RMM) installer (Atera, ScreenConnect, SimpleHelp, RemoteUtilities, Syncro, N-able, AnyDesk) which the operator then drives interactively as their primary C2. Detects msiexec/powershell/mshta downloading from a file-sharing host that's followed within minutes by an RMM agent first-execution on the same device.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("msiexec.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","outlook.exe","explorer.exe") (Processes.process_name IN ("ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","AteraAgent.exe","Atera_Agent.exe","Syncro.Installer.exe","SyncroService.exe","Kabuto.Installer.Actions.exe","SimpleHelpCustomer.exe","RemoteCustomer.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","AnyDesk.exe","TakeControl.exe","client32.exe","NetSupportSchool.exe","N-able.exe")) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as src | join type=inner src [ | tstats summariesonly=t values(All_Traffic.url) as fetched_url min(_time) as fetch_time from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app IN ("msiexec.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe") (All_Traffic.url="*onehub.com*" OR All_Traffic.url="*egnyte.com*" OR All_Traffic.url="*sync.com*" OR All_Traffic.url="*file.io*" OR All_Traffic.url="*filetransfer.io*" OR All_Traffic.url="*sendspace.com*" OR All_Traffic.url="*ufile.io*" OR All_Traffic.url="*wetransfer.com*" OR All_Traffic.url="*filebin.net*" OR All_Traffic.url="*mediafire.com*" OR All_Traffic.url="*1drv.ms*" OR All_Traffic.url="*dropbox.com*") by All_Traffic.src | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rename src as src ] | where firstTime - fetch_time < 600 AND firstTime - fetch_time > -60 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(fetch_time)
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let BaselineDays = 60d;
let RMMBins = dynamic(["screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","ateraagent.exe","atera_agent.exe","syncro.installer.exe","syncroservice.exe","kabuto.installer.actions.exe","simplehelpcustomer.exe","remotecustomer.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","anydesk.exe","takecontrol.exe","client32.exe","netsupportschool.exe","n-able.exe"]);
let FileShareHosts = dynamic(["onehub.com","egnyte.com","sync.com","file.io","filetransfer.io","sendspace.com","ufile.io","wetransfer.com","filebin.net","mediafire.com","1drv.ms","dropbox.com"]);
// 1. Brand-new RMM agent process — never seen on this device in baseline
let PriorRMM = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(BaselineDays) .. ago(LookbackDays))
    | where FileName has_any (RMMBins)
    | summarize by DeviceName, FileName;
let RMMFirstRun = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName has_any (RMMBins)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("msiexec.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","outlook.exe","explorer.exe")
    | join kind=leftanti PriorRMM on DeviceName, FileName
    | project RmmTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ParentImage=InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              RmmBin=FileName, RmmCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
// 2. Same device fetched a payload from a file-sharing host within 10 min
let FileShareFetch = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("msiexec.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe")
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (FileShareHosts)
    | project FetchTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl,
              FetchedBy=InitiatingProcessFileName,
              FetchCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
RMMFirstRun
| join kind=inner FileShareFetch on DeviceId
| where RmmTime between (FetchTime - 1m .. FetchTime + 10m)
| project RmmTime, FetchTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', RmmTime, FetchTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName, RemoteUrl, FetchedBy, FetchCmd,
          ParentImage, ParentCmd, RmmBin, RmmCmd
| order by RmmTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let BaselineDays = 60d;
let RMMBins = dynamic(["screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","ateraagent.exe","atera_agent.exe","syncro.installer.exe","syncroservice.exe","kabuto.installer.actions.exe","simplehelpcustomer.exe","remotecustomer.exe","rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","anydesk.exe","takecontrol.exe","client32.exe","netsupportschool.exe","n-able.exe"]);
let FileShareHosts = dynamic(["onehub.com","egnyte.com","sync.com","file.io","filetransfer.io","sendspace.com","ufile.io","wetransfer.com","filebin.net","mediafire.com","1drv.ms","dropbox.com"]);
let PriorRMM = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(BaselineDays) .. ago(LookbackDays))
    | where EventID == 4688
    | extend BinName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where BinName in (RMMBins)
    | summarize by Computer, BinName;
let RMMFirstRun = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend BinName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
             ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where BinName in (RMMBins)
    | where ParentBin in ("msiexec.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","outlook.exe","explorer.exe")
    | join kind=leftanti PriorRMM on Computer, BinName
    | project RmmTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, BinName;
let FileShareFetch = CommonSecurityLog
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where RequestURL has_any (FileShareHosts)
    | extend Computer = coalesce(SourceHostName, DeviceCustomString1)
    | project FetchTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, RequestURL, SourceIP, DestinationIP;
RMMFirstRun
| join kind=inner FileShareFetch on Computer
| where RmmTime between (FetchTime - 1m .. FetchTime + 10m)
| project RmmTime, FetchTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', RmmTime, FetchTime),
          Computer, Account, RequestURL,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine
| order by RmmTime desc
ucs.rationale: MuddyWater (MERCURY / Mango Sandstorm / Static Kitten) has been Microsoft- and Mandiant-tracked since 2021 as the most prolific abuser of legitimate RMM tooling for state-sponsored intrusion — they reuse this exact chain across Israeli, Saudi, Turkish, and US-targeted operations. Generic 'PowerShell from Word' detections miss them because they avoid custom malware in favour of signed RMM binaries;

ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater PowGoop loader: signed GoogleUpdate.exe side-loading malicious goopdate.dll outside Program Files
ucs.description: Hunts MuddyWater's signature loader — the 'PowGoop' family — which drops a legitimate Microsoft- or Google-signed GoogleUpdate.exe alongside an attacker-supplied goopdate.dll and an encrypted .dat config in a non-standard directory (commonly %ProgramData%, %TEMP%, or a per-user path). The genuine binary side-loads the malicious DLL on launch, which decrypts the .dat into a PowerShell stager. This combination is uniquely MuddyWater and has been tracked by Mandiant, Cisco Talos, and DeepInstinct.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.process_path) as image_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="GoogleUpdate.exe" NOT (Processes.process_path="*\\Program Files\\Google\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Program Files (x86)\\Google\\*") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as src | join type=inner src [ | tstats summariesonly=t values(Filesystem.file_path) as dll_path values(Filesystem.file_name) as dll_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="goopdate.dll" NOT (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Program Files\\Google\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Program Files (x86)\\Google\\*") by host Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename host as src ] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// PowGoop side-load: legitimate GoogleUpdate.exe loading goopdate.dll from a non-Google path.
// MuddyWater's loader stages the trio (GoogleUpdate.exe + goopdate.dll + goopdate.dat) into
// a writable directory and runs the binary, which side-loads the rogue DLL.
let LegitGooglePaths = dynamic([@"c:\program files\google\", @"c:\program files (x86)\google\"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "googleupdate.exe"
| where FileName =~ "goopdate.dll"
| extend ImagePath = tolower(FolderPath),
         LoaderPath = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
| where not(ImagePath startswith LegitGooglePaths[0])
   and not(ImagePath startswith LegitGooglePaths[1])
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where FileName in~ ("goopdate.dat","goopdate.dll")
    | project DatTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DroppedName=FileName, DroppedPath=FolderPath,
              DropperProcess=InitiatingProcessFileName,
              DropperCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine
  ) on DeviceId
| where isnull(DatTime) or DatTime between (Timestamp - 1d .. Timestamp + 1m)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          LoaderPath, ImagePath, SHA256,
          InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          DroppedName, DroppedPath, DropperProcess, DropperCmd
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sysmon EID 7 (ImageLoad) is required — Defender-collected SecurityEvent does not carry image-load.
let LegitGooglePaths = dynamic([@"c:\program files\google\", @"c:\program files (x86)\google\"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon"
| where EventID == 7
| extend Image = tolower(tostring(EventData.Image)),
         ImageLoaded = tolower(tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded)),
         User = tostring(EventData.User),
         Signed = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         Signature = tostring(EventData.Signature)
| where Image endswith "\\googleupdate.exe"
| where ImageLoaded endswith "\\goopdate.dll"
| where not(ImageLoaded startswith LegitGooglePaths[0])
   and not(ImageLoaded startswith LegitGooglePaths[1])
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User, Image, ImageLoaded, Signed, Signature
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: MuddyWater PowGoop Loader - GoogleUpdate.exe Side-Loading goopdate.dll Outside Program Files
id: 8c4f2e6a-3f9c-4f7b-9b1d-muddyw-powgoop
status: experimental
description: Detects the MuddyWater 'PowGoop' loader chain - a legitimate signed GoogleUpdate.exe loading a goopdate.dll from a non-standard (non Program Files\Google) location, which is the actor's signature DLL side-loading mechanism for staging PowerShell payloads.
references:
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/mandiant-uncovers-cluster-iranian-threat-activity
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/muddywater-targets-organizations-in-israel/
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2022/new-muddywater-threat-old-kitten-new-tricks/
author: detection-engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0069
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_loader:
    Image|endswith: '\GoogleUpdate.exe'
    ImageLoaded|endswith: '\goopdate.dll'
  filter_legit_path:
    ImageLoaded|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\Google\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\'
  condition: selection_loader and not filter_legit_path
falsepositives:
  - Portable Google updater redistributed in non-standard installs (rare; verify signature and parent path)
level: high
ucs.rationale: PowGoop is a near-fingerprint-grade indicator of MuddyWater (G0069 / MERCURY / Earth Vetala / Static Kitten) — Mandiant, Cisco Talos and Check Point have all attributed the goopdate.dll + goopdate.dat + GoogleUpdate.exe trio exclusively to this Iranian MOIS-aligned crew, used against Middle East government, telecom, and energy targets. The legitimate Google updater is Microsoft-signed and Defender
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
